Going on everyone happy, well happy day, whatever day you are watching this. So I decided to make a video, this one, that is essentially the crash course of crash courses for supplement sales. I think that you will make phenomenally more money if you put four hours towards everything in supplement selling secrets. But people prioritize their time differently and still watch Netflix and I don't want to pretend like I live in a bubble. So the intention of this video is to give you a 15, 20 minute breakdown that gives you everything that, or the 80, 20, the 80% of what you would need to know to sell supplements at a very high percentage. And so if you were at one of my gyms and you were brand new and you were about to see somebody in 15 minutes, what would I tell you to increase the likelihood that that person would actually buy? The first thing is, and I'll get into the actual, like what to say in a second, is kind of the perspective from which you are approaching the customer and the program. So most people who are going to be buying supplements are going to be people who are starting a program, whether that's an internal challenge or it's somebody who's a new customer who's coming in. Either way, the mindset that you should be approaching the sale is from is that the supplements are part of the program. It's not like a nice to have or something that you were trying to sell. It's like, so these are the workouts, this is your food plan and these are the supplements you need to take, period. And honestly, I had guys who started working for me after we were already selling supplements and whatnot. And when they would come in, I wouldn't even tell them that it was a sale because I didn't want to stress them out. I would just say, okay, so after you go over the nutrition plan, all you're going to do is ask them what flavors they're going to take. You're going to process out the things they're not going to need and then you're going to ask them and then you're going to say, hey, is it cool if we put that on the card that you have on file? That's it. Okay. And these are the four that we want them to take for the most part. And I would just tell them that and I'd have guys closing it 100% because they didn't know that it was a sale. So I don't want you to psych yourself out or psych your team out by putting a lot of pressure on it because it's a low pressure sale. It should be a low pressure sale. People want to buy supplements. They're going to buy them anyways and they might as well buy the right ones and they might as well buy the ones that are amazing and that will actually help them. So that is kind of the precursor to this conversation is that they shouldn't feel like they have an option to not buy the supplements. They really shouldn't feel that way. And people, if you say, hey, you need to take, you can even hear the words. I'm like, you need to take this, this and this. They should just not be like, okay, cool. And most people will because you're not asking. You're prescribing and it's a fundamental difference in your prescribing from a position of expertise. And the beautiful thing about a supplement sale is it is not the same as a cold traffic sale. So cold traffic sale, someone walks in the door, they're not a customer yet and then you're trying to get them to become a customer. The difference is that all of the supplement sales are selling to existing customers. They are selling to people who have already given you money, which means the likelihood that like you should know that people who are most likely to give you money are people who have already given you money in the past. And so it is an upsell to an existing customer, which has totally different metrics than a cold traffic acquisition, which is how you can get 85, 90, 95, 100% close rates is because it's not the same sale. These people have already committed to the program. They already want to do that. They're excited. It's before they started, but they're like mentally ready. And at that point you want to maximize the likelihood of success and you're going to explain to them, you're going to provide tons of value upfront so that they feel like they've already gotten some stuff from what they've paid for.
